author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter VIII. 28–32.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of the holy Gospel according to John, which you see in our hand, your Charity has already heard much, whereon by God’s grace we have discoursed according to our ability, pressing on your notice that this evangelist, specially, has chosen to speak of the Lord’s divinity, wherein He is equal with the Father and the only Son of God; and on that account he has been compared to the eagle, because no other bird is understood to take a loftier flight.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, to what follows in order, as the Lord enables us to treat of it, listen with all your attention.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We have spoken to you on the preceding passage, suggesting how the Father may be understood as True, and the Son as the Truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when the Lord Jesus said, “He that sent me is true,” the Jews understood not that He spake to them of the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And He said to them, as you have just heard in the reading, “When ye have lifted up the Son of man, then shall ye know that I am, and [that] I do nothing of myself; but as the Father hath taught me, I speak these things.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What means this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it looks as if all He said was, that they would know who He was after His passion.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Without doubt, therefore, He saw that some there, whom He Himself knew, whom with the rest of His saints He Himself in His foreknowledge had chosen before the foundation of the world, would believe after His passion.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: These are the very persons whom we are constantly commending, and with much entreaty setting forth for your imitation.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For on the sending down of the Holy Spirit after the Lord’s passion, and resurrection, and ascension, when miracles were being done in the name of Him whom, as if dead, the persecuting Jews had despised, they were pricked in their hearts; and they who in their rage slew Him were changed and believed; and they who in their rage shed His blood, now in the spirit of faith drank it; to wit, those three thousand, and those five thousand Jews whom now He saw there, when He said, “When ye have lifted up the Son of man, then shall ye know that I am [He].”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was as if He had said, I let your recognition lie over till I have completed my passion: in your own order ye shall know who I am.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not that all who heard Him were only then to believe, that is, after the Lord’s passion; for a little after it is said, “As He spake these words, many believed on Him;” and the Son of man was not yet lifted up.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the lifting up He is speaking of is that of His passion, not of His glorification; of the cross, not of heaven; for He was exalted there also when He hung on the tree.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But that exaltation was His humiliation; for then He became obedient even to the death of the cross.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This required to be accomplished by the hands of those who should afterwards believe, and to whom He says, “When ye have lifted up the Son of man, then shall ye know that I am [He].”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And why so, but that no one might despair, however guilty his conscience, when he saw those forgiven their homicide who had slain the Christ?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord then, recognizing such in that crowd, said, “When ye have lifted up the Son of man, then shall ye know that I am [He].”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You know already what “I am” signifies; and we must not be continually repeating, lest so great a subject beget distaste.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Recall that, “I am who am,” and “He who is hath sent me,” and you will recognize the meaning of the words, “Then shall ye know that I am.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But both the Father is, and the Holy Spirit is.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To the same is belongs the whole Trinity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But because the Lord spake as the Son, in order that, when He says, “Then shall ye know that I am,” there might be no chance of entrance for the error of the Sabellians, that is, of the Patripassians,—an error which I have charged you not to hold, but to beware of,—the error, I mean, of those who have said, The Father and Son are one and the same; two names, but one reality;—to guard them against that error, when the Lord said, “Then shall ye know that I am,” that He might not be understood as Himself the Father, He immediately added, “And I do nothing of myself; but as my Father taught me, I speak these things.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Already was the Sabellian beginning to rejoice over the discovery of a ground for his error; but immediately on showing himself as it were in the shade, he was confounded by the light of the following sentence.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou thoughtest that He was the Father, because He said, “I am.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear now that He is the Son: “And I do nothing of myself.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What means this, “I do nothing of myself”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of myself I am not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Son is God, of the Father; but the Father is God, yet not of the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Son is God of God, and the Father is God, but not of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Son is light of light; and the Father is light, but not of light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Son is, but there is [One] of whom He is; and the Father is, but there is none of whom He is.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let not then, my brethren, His further words, “As my Father hath taught me, I speak these things,” be the occasion of any carnal thought stealing into your minds.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For human weakness cannot think, but as it is accustomed to act and to hear.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do not then set before your eyes as it were two men, one the father, the other the son, and the father speaking to the son; as any one of you may do, when you say something to your son, admonishing and instructing him how to speak, to charge his memory with what you have told him, and, having done so, to express it in words, to enunciate distinctly, and convey to the ears of others what he has apprehended with his own.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Think not thus, lest you be fabricating idols in your heart.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The human shape, the outlines of human limbs, the form of human flesh, the outward senses, stature and motions of the body, the functions of the tongue, the distinctions of sounds,—think not of such as existing in that Trinity, save as they pertain to the servant-form, which the only-begotten Son assumed, when the Word was made flesh to dwell among us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thereof I forbid thee not, human weakness, to think according to thy knowledge: nay, rather I require thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If the faith that is in thee be true, think of Christ as such; but as such of the Virgin Mary, not of God the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He was an infant, He grew as a man, He walked as a man, He hungered, He thirsted as a man, He slept as a man; at last He suffered as a man, hung on the tree, was slain and buried as a man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the same form He rose again; in the same, before the eyes of His disciples, He ascended into heaven; in the same will He yet come to judgment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For angel lips have declared in the Gospel, “He shall so come in like manner as ye have seen Him go into heaven.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When then you think of the servant-form in Christ, think of a human likeness, if you have faith; but when you think, “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God,” away with all human fashioning from your heart.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Banish from your thoughts everything bounded by corporeal limits, included in local measurement, or spread out in a mass, how great soever its size.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Perish utterly such a figment from your heart.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Think, if you can, on the beauty of wisdom, picture to yourself the beauty of righteousness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Has that a shape? a size? a color?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It has none of these, and yet it is; for if it were not, it would neither be loved nor worthy of praise, nor be cherished in our heart and life as an object of honor and affection.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But men here become wise; and whence would they so, had wisdom no existence?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And further, O man, if thou canst not see thine own wisdom with the eyes of the flesh, nor think of it by the same mental imagery as thou canst of bodily things, wilt thou dare to thrust the shape of a human body on the wisdom of God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What shall we say then, brethren?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How spake the Father to the Son, seeing that the Son says, “As the Father taught me, I speak these things”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Did He speak to Him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When the Father taught the Son, did He use words, as you do when you teach your son?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How could He use words to the Word!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What words, many in number, could be used to the one Word?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Did the Word of the Father approach His ears to the Father’s mouth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such things are carnal: banish them from your hearts.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this I say, if only you have understood my words, I certainly have spoken and my words have sounded, and by their sound have reached your ears, and through your sense of hearing have carried their meaning to your mind, if so be you have understood.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Suppose that some person of Latin speech has heard, but has only heard without understanding, what I have said.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As regards the noise issuing from my mouth, he who has understood not has been a sharer therein just like yourselves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He has heard that sound; the same syllables have smote on his ears, but they have produced no effect on his mind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because he understood not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if you have understood, whence comes your understanding?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: My words have sounded in the ear: have I kindled any light in the heart?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Without doubt, if what I have said is true, and this truth you have not only heard, but also understood, two things have there been wrought (distinguish between them), hearing and intelligence.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hearing has been wrought by me, but by whom has understanding?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I have spoken to the ear, that you might hear; who has spoken to your heart for understanding?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Doubtless some one has also said something to your heart, that not only the noise of words might strike your ear, but something also of the truth might descend into your heart.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Some one has spoken also to your heart, but you do not see him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, brethren, you have understood, your heart also has been spoken to.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Intelligence is the gift of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And who, if you have understood, has spoken so in your heart, but He to whom the Psalm says, “Give me understanding, that I may learn Thy commandments?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For example, the bishop has spoken.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What has he said, some one asks.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You repeat what he has spoken, and add, He has said the truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then another, who has not understood, says, What has he said, or what is it you are praising?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Both have heard me; I have spoken to both; but to one of them God has spoken.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If we may compare small things with great (for what are we to Him?), something, I know not what, of an incorporeal and spiritual kind God works in us, which is neither sound to strike the ear, nor color to be discerned by the eyes, nor smell to enter the nostrils, nor taste to be judged of by the mouth, nor anything hard or soft to be sensible to the touch; yet something there is which it is easy to feel,—impossible to explain.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If then God, as I was saying, speaks in our hearts without sound, how speaks He to His Son?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus then, brethren, think thus as much as you can, if, as I have said, we may in some measure compare small things with great: think thus.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In an incorporeal way the Father spoke to the Son, because in an incorporeal way the Father begot the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nor did He so teach Him as if He had begotten Him untaught; but to have taught Him is the same as to have begotten Him full of knowledge; and this, “The Father hath taught me,” is the same as, The Father hath begotten me already knowing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if, as few understand, the nature of the Truth is simple, to be is to the Son the same as to know.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: From Him therefore He has knowledge, from whom He has being.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not that from Him He had first being, and afterwards knowledge; but as in begetting He gave Him to be, so in begetting He gave Him to know; for, as was said, to the simple nature of the Truth, being is not one thing and knowing another, but one and the same.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus then He spoke to the Jews, and added, “And He that sent me is with me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He had already said this also before, but of this important point He is constantly reminding them,—“He sent me,” and “He is with me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If then, O Lord, He is with Thee, not so much hath the One been sent by the other, but ye Both have come.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet, while Both are together, One was sent, the Other was the sender; for incarnation is a sending, and the incarnation itself belongs only to the Son and not to the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Father therefore sent the Son, but did not withdraw from the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it was not that the Father was absent from the place to which He sent the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For where is not the Maker of all things?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Where is He not, who said, “I fill heaven and earth”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But perhaps the Father is everywhere, and the Son not so?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Listen to the evangelist: “He was in this world, and the world was made by Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore said He, “He that sent me,” by whose power as Father I am incarnate, “is with me,—hath not left me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why hath He not left me?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He hath not left me,” He says, “alone; for I do always those things that please Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That equality exists always; not from a certain beginning, and then onwards; but without beginning, without end.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For Divine generation has no beginning in time, since time itself was created by the Only-begotten.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “As He spake these words, many believed on Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Would that, while I speak also, many, who before this were otherwise disposed, understood and believed on Him!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For perhaps there are some Arians in this large assembly.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I dare not suspect that there are any Sabellians, who say that the Father Himself is one with the Son, seeing that heresy is too old, and has been gradually eviscerated.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But that of the Arians seems still to have some movement about it, like that of a putrefying carcase, or certainly, at the most, like a man at the last gasp; and from this some still require deliverance, just as from that other many were delivered.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This province, indeed, did not use to have such; but ever since the arrival of many foreigners, some of these have also found their way to our neighborhood.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See then, while the Lord spoke these words, many Jews believed on Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: May I see also that, while I am speaking, Arians are believing, not on me, but with me!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Then said the Lord to those Jews who believed on Him, If ye continue in my word.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Continue,” I say, for you are now initiated and have begun to be there.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “If ye continue,” that is, in the faith which is now begun in you who believe, to what will you attain?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See the nature of the beginning, and whither it leads.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You have loved the foundation, give heed to the summit, and out of this low condition seek that other elevation.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For faith has humility, but knowledge and immortality and eternity possess not lowliness, but loftiness; that is, upraising, all-sufficiency, eternal stability, full freedom from hostile assault, from fear of failure.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That which has its beginning in faith is great, but is despised.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In a building also the foundation is usually of little account with the unskilled.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A large trench is made, and stones are thrown in every way and everywhere.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: No embellishment, no beauty are apparent there; just as also in the root of a tree there is no appearance of beauty.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet all that delights you in the tree has sprung from the root.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You look at the root and feel no delight: you look at the tree and admire it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Foolish man! what you admire has grown out of that which gave you no delight.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The faith of believers seems a thing of little value,—you have no scales to weigh it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear then to what it attains, and see its greatness: as the Lord Himself says in another place, “If ye have faith as a grain of mustard seed.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is there of less account than that, yet what is there pervaded with greater energy?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What more minute, yet what more fervidly expansive?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so “ye” also, He says, “if ye continue in my word,” wherein ye have believed, to what will ye be brought? “ye shall be my disciples indeed.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what does that benefit us? “and ye shall know the truth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, brethren, does He promise believers?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And ye shall know the truth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Had they not come to such knowledge when the Lord was speaking?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If they had not, how did they believe?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They believed, not because they knew, but that they might come to know.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For we believe in order that we may know, we do not know in order that we may believe.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what we shall yet know, neither eye hath seen, nor ear heard, nor hath it entered the heart of man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what is faith, but believing what you see not?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Faith then is to believe what you see not; truth, to see what you have believed, as He Himself saith in a certain place.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord then walked on earth, first of all, for the creation of faith.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He was man, He was made in a low condition.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He was seen by all, but not by all was He known.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By many was He rejected, by the multitude was He slain, by few was He mourned; and yet even by those who mourned Him, His true being was still unrecognized.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All this is the beginning as it were of faith’s lineaments and future up-building.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As the Lord, referring thereto, saith in a certain place, “He that loveth me keepeth my commandments; and he that loveth me shall be loved of my Father, and I will love him, and will manifest myself to him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They certainly already saw the person to whom they were listening; and yet to them, if they loved Him, does He give it as a promise that they should see Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So also here, “Ye shall know the truth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is that not the truth which Thou hast been speaking?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The truth it is, but as yet it is only believed, not beheld.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If you abide in that which is believed, you shall attain to that which is seen.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence John himself, the holy evangelist, says in his epistle, “Dearly beloved, we are the sons of God; but it is not yet apparent what we shall be.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We are so already, and something we shall be.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What more shall we be than we are?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Listen: “It is not yet apparent what we shall be: [but] we know that, when He shall appear, we shall be like Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For we shall see Him as He is.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A great promise, but the reward of faith.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You seek the reward; then let the work precede.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If you believe, ask for the reward of faith; but if you believe not, with what face can you seek the reward of faith?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “If” then “ye continue in my word, ye shall be my disciples indeed,” that ye may behold the very truth as it is, not through sounding words, but in dazzling light, wherewith He shall satisfy us: as we read in the psalm, “The light of Thy countenance is impressed upon us.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We are God’s money: we have wandered away as coin from the treasury.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The impression that was stamped upon us has been rubbed out by our wandering.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He has come to refashion, for He it was that fashioned us at first; and He is Himself asking for His money, as Cæsar for his.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore He says, “Render unto Cæsar the things that are Cæsar’s, and unto God the things that are God’s:” to Cæsar his money, to God yourselves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And then shall the truth be reproduced in us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What shall I say to your Charity?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Oh that our hearts were in some measure aspiring after that ineffable glory!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Oh that we were passing our pilgrimage in sighs, and loving not the world, and continually pushing onwards with pious minds to Him who hath called us!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Longing is the very bosom of the heart.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We shall attain, if with all our power we give way to our longing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such in our behalf is the object of the divine Scriptures, of the assembling of the people, of the celebration of the sacra ments, of holy baptism, of singing God’s praise, and of this our own exposition,—that this longing may not only be implanted and germinate, but also expand to such a measure of capacity as to be fit to take in what eye hath not seen, nor ear heard, nor hath entered into the heart of man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But love with me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He who loves God is not much in love with money.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And I have but touched on this infirmity, not venturing to say, He loves not money at all, but, He loves not money much; as if money were to be loved, but not in a great degree.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Oh, were we loving God worthily, we should have no love at all for money!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Money then will be thy means of pilgrimage, not the stimulant of lust; something to use for necessity, not to joy over as a means of delight.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Love God, if He has wrought in thee somewhat of that which thou hearest and praisest.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Use the world: let not the world hold thee captive.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou art passing on the journey thou hast begun; thou hast come, again to depart, not to abide.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou art passing on thy journey, and this life is but a wayside inn.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Use money as the traveller at an inn uses table, cup, pitcher, and couch, with the purpose not of remaining, but of leaving them behind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If such you would be, you, who can stir up your hearts and hear me; if such you would be, you will attain to His promises.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is not too much for your strength, for mighty is the hand of Him who hath called you.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He hath called you.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Call upon Him, say to Him, Thou hast called us, we call upon Thee; see, we have heard Thee calling us, hear us calling upon Thee: lead us whither Thou hast promised; perfect what Thou hast begun; forsake not Thine own gifts; leave not Thine own field; let Thy tender shoots yet be gathered into Thy barn.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Temptations abound in the world, but greater is He who made the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Temptations abound, but he fails not whose hope reposes in Him in whom there is no deficiency.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I have been exhorting you, brethren, to this in such words, because the freedom of which our Lord Jesus Christ speaks belongs not to this present time.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Look at what He added: “Ye shall be my disciples indeed; and ye shall know the truth, and the truth shall set you free.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What means that—“shall set you free”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It shall make you freemen.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In a word, the carnal, and fleshly-minded Jews—not those who had believed, but those in the crowd who believed not—thought that an injury was done them, because He said to them, “The truth shall make you free.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They were indignant at being designated as slaves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And slaves truly they were; and He explains to them what slavery it is, and what is that future freedom which is promised by Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But of this liberty and of that slavery it were too long to speak to-day.
